fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 33-year-old White female with schizophrenia for routine check-in.
fields.Subjective: Patient presented appearing cheerful and engaged. Reports excellent adherence to cariprazine 4.5 mg every morning with breakfast, never misses a dose. States feeling fantastic. Denies any hallucinations or paranoid thoughts for past seven months. Sleep great at 7-8 hours nightly. Appetite excellent, weight healthy at 138 lbs. Working full-time as elementary school teacher, loves her students and job. Mood 'fantastic' rated 9/10. Lives alone in townhouse. Training for half-marathon. Active social life with friends and book club. Attending therapy monthly for wellness. Two years sober from all substances. Engaged to be married next spring. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Evaluated by Jennifer Park, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Young adult in casual summer dress, excellent grooming, vibrant affect

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizophrenia, in sustained full remission with excellent functioning.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Living independently; employed as teacher; two years sober; active athletic and social life; engaged to be married.
Assessment_and_Plan: Excellent medication adherence and sustained remission.
Commended on recovery and upcoming marriage.
fields.Author: Note written by Jennifer Park, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner — Educator Mental Health Services, 8821 School District Plaza, Minneapolis, MN 55401, Phone: (555) 338-9954, Fax: (555) 338-9955
